tables.grain: One row per Blizzard guild member character.

columns.name: total_raids_tracked
columns.description: Total WCL raid reports in attendance data

columns.name: raids_present
columns.description: Reports where player was marked present

columns.name: possible_main
columns.description: Character's own name (alt resolution handled in gold_raid_team)

tables.grain: One row per canonical player name observed in WCL logs, enriched with guild metadata when available.
tables.description: Canonical per-character dimension. One row per player_name observed in WCL logs, enriched with Blizzard guild rank when the character matches the guild roster. Use this as the join target for any fact table that has only a player_name. For Mythic+ identity (which keys on realm) use the player_identity_key on the Mythic+ products instead.
tables.ai_summary: Player identity dimension. Use to filter to guild members (`is_guild_member = true`) or the active raid team (`is_raid_team = true`) and to look up rank labels for display. Joins to almost every fact table on `player_name`.
example_questions: Who is on the active raid team?
List all guild members and their ranks.
Which characters first appeared in raid logs after a given date?
How many players are in each rank category?
not_recommended_for: Mythic+ joins — Mythic+ products use `player_identity_key`, not `player_name` alone.
Counting raid attendance — use `gold_player_attendance` (presence-aware) instead.

columns.name: player_class
columns.description: WoW class (from actor roster; falls back to performance data)

columns.name: is_guild_member
columns.description: Whether matched in Blizzard guild roster

columns.name: rank
columns.description: Guild rank ID (null for non-guild players)

columns.name: is_raid_team
columns.description: Whether rank is in the active raid team (ranks 0–5, 8)

tables.grain: One row per player death event.
tables.description: One row per player death event across all WCL reports. The grain is the death itself, not the fight, so a player can have many rows in a single fight. Includes the killing-blow ability when WCL recorded one. Use this for "who dies most", "what kills us most often", and per-ability death breakdowns. Wipe rate / pull counts live in gold_boss_wipe_analysis.
tables.ai_summary: Death-event fact. Each row is a single death; group by player_name to count deaths, by killing_blow_name to find which mechanics kill most, by encounter to find dangerous fights. Joins to dim_player on player_name and to gold_encounter_catalog on (zone_id, encounter_id) — but encounter_id is not on this table directly, derive from a join on (report_code, fight_id) via fact_player_fight_performance if needed.
example_questions: Who dies most often on each boss?
What are our most common killing blows?
Which abilities killed players the most last raid night?
Show death counts per player on Mythic <boss>.

metrics.name: death_count
metrics.definition: Count of rows per (player, fight) or per (boss, ability).
metrics.caveats: A single fight may have multiple rows per player when the player died, was rezzed, and died again.

metrics.name: most_common_killing_blow
metrics.definition: killing_blow_name with the highest count for the chosen scope.
metrics.caveats: Null killing_blow_name means a friendly source or empty events array — exclude with WHERE killing_blow_name IS NOT NULL.

not_recommended_for: Counting wipes — a wipe and a death are different things; use gold_boss_wipe_analysis.
Computing parse percentiles — use fact_player_fight_performance.
Treating null killing_blow_name as zero — exclude or label as "unknown".

columns.name: overkill
columns.description: Overkill damage amount on the killing blow

columns.name: killing_blow_name
columns.description: Name of the ability that killed the player (nullable — null if killing blow was from a friendly or events array was empty)

columns.name: killing_blow_id
columns.description: Spell ID of the killing blow ability

tables.grain: One row per player on each tracked kill fight.
tables.description: Primary fact table for raid performance analysis. One row per player per boss-kill fight, joining WCL player performance, fight events, and player rankings. Includes throughput, parse percentiles, item level, consumables, and secondary stats. Wipe pulls are not included here — use gold_boss_wipe_analysis or gold_wipe_survival_discipline for wipes.
tables.ai_summary: Per-player, per-kill performance facts. Use for "best players on boss X", "parse percentiles", "throughput trends", "consumable coverage on kills". For wipes / deaths / survival, use gold_boss_wipe_analysis or gold_wipe_survival_discipline instead — this table only contains kill rows.
example_questions: Which players have the best parse percentiles on Mythic <boss>?
What is the average DPS per fight on heroic <boss> by player?
Who is improving over time on <boss> by parse percentile?
Which players consistently use flasks and food on kill fights?

metrics.name: throughput_per_second
metrics.definition: Role-aware DPS for dps/tank or HPS for healer, sourced from WCL rankings.amount.
metrics.caveats: Null when WCL has no ranking entry for that player/fight.

metrics.name: rank_percent
metrics.definition: WCL parse percentile (0–100), role-aware.
metrics.caveats: Often null for recent reports until rankings finalise; never bucket null as zero.

not_recommended_for: Counting wipes — this table is filtered to kills (is_kill=true).
Death analysis — use fact_player_events for deaths.
Bucketing nullable parse fields as zero.

columns.name: is_kill
columns.description: Always true (table filters to kills only)

columns.name: avg_item_level
columns.description: Average item level of equipped gear

columns.name: combat_potion_casts
columns.description: Number of damage/health combat-potion casts (DPS/healer combat usage signal).

columns.name: combat_potion_names
columns.description: Comma-separated list of combat-potion names cast (raw aura/cast names).

columns.name: has_food_buff
columns.description: Whether a recognised raid food buff was active during the fight.

columns.name: food_buff_names
columns.description: Comma-separated raid food buff names that were active.

columns.name: has_flask_or_phial_buff
columns.description: Whether a flask or phial buff was active during the fight.

columns.name: flask_or_phial_names
columns.description: Comma-separated flask/phial buff names that were active.

columns.name: has_weapon_enhancement
columns.description: Whether a weapon enhancement (e.g. rune/stone/oil/imbue) was applied.

columns.name: weapon_enhancement_names
columns.description: Comma-separated weapon enhancement names that were applied.

columns.name: throughput_per_second
columns.description: Role-aware throughput from WCL rankings.amount: DPS for dps/tank rows, HPS for healer rows (nullable — null when no WCL ranking for this fight/player)

columns.name: rank_percent
columns.description: WCL parse percentile (0–100), role-aware (DPS parse for dps/tank, HPS parse for healer)

tables.grain: One row per encounter, boss, zone, and difficulty with at least one kill.
One row per player on each tracked boss kill.
One row per boss encounter, boss name, zone, and difficulty with wipe mechanics metrics.
tables.description: Per-(encounter, difficulty) wipe-mechanics breakdown. Reports the share of wipes by phase bucket (P1, P2, P3+), wipes by duration bucket (<1m, 1–3m, 3–5m, 5m+), and a progress_trend that compares the last week average wipe HP% to the all-time average. Use for "where are we wiping on this boss" (phase) and "are we improving" (trend).
tables.ai_summary: Per-encounter wipe diagnosis. Use for "which phase do we wipe in most", "are we trending closer to a kill on <boss>", or "do we have lots of fast/early wipes". Pair with gold_boss_wipe_analysis for HP% and best-pull detail.
example_questions: Which phase are we wiping in most on Mythic <boss>?
Are we improving on <boss> this week?
Which bosses have the most fast (<1 minute) wipes?
What share of <boss> wipes reach phase 3?

metrics.name: progress_trend
metrics.definition: avg_boss_pct minus last_week_avg_boss_pct.
metrics.caveats: Positive = recent wipes are closer to kill. Null when there are no last-week wipes; do not interpret null as "no progress".

not_recommended_for: Counting kills — this is wipe-only.
Player-level analysis — no player columns.
Comparing absolute wipe counts across bosses with vastly different attempt counts (use ratios).

columns.name: total_wipes
columns.description: Total wipes counted in this rollup.

columns.name: avg_boss_pct
columns.description: Average boss HP% remaining at wipe (closer to 0 = closer to a kill).

columns.name: pct_wipes_phase_1
columns.description: Share of wipes (0–100) that ended in phase 1.

columns.name: pct_wipes_phase_2
columns.description: Share of wipes (0–100) that ended in phase 2.

columns.name: pct_wipes_phase_3_plus
columns.description: Share of wipes (0–100) that ended in phase 3 or later.

columns.name: last_week_avg_boss_pct
columns.description: Average wipe boss HP% over the last 7 days; null when no recent wipes.

columns.name: progress_trend
columns.description: all-time avg_boss_pct minus last_week_avg_boss_pct; positive = recent wipes are closer to kill.

tables.grain: One row per report, boss encounter, and difficulty with nightly progression summary.
One row per encounter, boss, zone, and difficulty.
tables.description: Per-encounter, per-difficulty progression rollup. Includes total pulls, kills, wipes, best kill time, and a wipe-to-kill ratio for each boss the guild has engaged. Use this as the primary progression scoreboard. For raid-night-level rollups use gold_raid_summary; for per-pull detail use gold_boss_pull_history; for wipe-only diagnosis use gold_boss_wipe_analysis.
tables.ai_summary: Per-encounter progression scoreboard. Rows show how many pulls/kills/wipes per boss-difficulty pair, with best kill time and wipe ratio. Filter by difficulty_label and zone_name for tier-specific views.
example_questions: Which bosses have we killed on Mythic?
Which bosses are we wiping on most?
Show progression on the current tier ordered by wipe-to-kill ratio.
When did we first kill <boss> on Heroic?
Which Mythic bosses have we attempted but not killed?

metrics.name: wipe_to_kill_ratio
metrics.definition: total_wipes / max(total_kills, 1).
metrics.caveats: For unkilled bosses, denominator is 1, so the ratio equals total_wipes.

metrics.name: best_kill_seconds
metrics.definition: Fastest successful kill at this difficulty.
metrics.caveats: Null when the boss has never been killed at this difficulty.

not_recommended_for: Per-night attendance — use gold_raid_summary or gold_player_attendance.
Per-pull mechanics analysis — use gold_boss_pull_history or gold_boss_mechanics.
Player-level diagnosis — this table has no player columns.

columns.name: best_kill_seconds
columns.description: Fastest kill duration recorded; null if never killed.

columns.name: avg_pull_duration_seconds
columns.description: Average pull duration across all pulls (kills and wipes).

columns.name: is_killed
columns.description: Whether the boss has ever been killed at this difficulty.

columns.name: first_kill_date
columns.description: Date of the first kill at this difficulty; null if never killed.

columns.name: last_attempt_date
columns.description: Date of the most recent attempt (kill or wipe).

columns.name: wipe_to_kill_ratio
columns.description: total_wipes / max(total_kills, 1) — higher means more attempts per kill.

tables.grain: One row per tracked boss pull.
One row per boss encounter and difficulty with tracked wipes.
tables.description: Per-(encounter, difficulty) wipe rollup. Includes how many wipes, best (lowest remaining HP%) wipe, average wipe HP%, max phase reached, and wipe durations. Use this to answer "where are we wiping?" and "are we improving on this boss?". For per-pull mechanics use gold_boss_mechanics; for player survival diagnosis use gold_wipe_survival_discipline.
tables.ai_summary: Wipe-side counterpart to gold_boss_progression. Use for "where are we wiping?", "what is our best pull on <boss>?", "are we making progress (avg_wipe_pct trending down)?". Lower best_wipe_pct = closer to killing.
example_questions: Which bosses are we wiping on most this tier?
What is our best pull (lowest HP%) on Mythic <boss>?
How many raid nights have we spent on <boss>?
Which Mythic bosses are we farthest from killing?

metrics.name: best_wipe_pct
metrics.definition: Lowest boss HP% remaining across all wipes — primary "how close are we" signal.
metrics.caveats: Phase-gated bosses can show low pct on phase 1 wipes that aren't actually progress.

metrics.name: avg_wipe_pct
metrics.definition: Mean boss HP% remaining across wipes.
metrics.caveats: Sensitive to a small number of bad early-phase pulls; check max_phase_reached for context.

not_recommended_for: Counting kills — use gold_boss_progression.total_kills.
Per-pull or per-player analysis — use gold_boss_pull_history or gold_wipe_survival_discipline.
Computing parse percentiles — wipes don't generate parses.

columns.name: total_wipes
columns.description: Number of wipes recorded against this (encounter, difficulty).

columns.name: best_wipe_pct
columns.description: Lowest remaining boss HP% across all wipes (closer to 0 = closer to a kill).

columns.name: avg_wipe_pct
columns.description: Average remaining boss HP% across wipes.

columns.name: avg_last_phase
columns.description: Average final phase reached across wipes.

columns.name: max_phase_reached
columns.description: Furthest phase ever reached on a wipe.

columns.name: first_wipe_date
columns.description: Date of the first recorded wipe at this (encounter, difficulty).

columns.name: latest_wipe_date
columns.description: Date of the most recent wipe.

columns.name: raid_nights_attempted
columns.description: Distinct raid nights with at least one pull on this (encounter, difficulty).

tables.grain: One row per active raid-zone encounter reference.
tables.description: Reference dimension of active raid encounters. One row per (zone_id, encounter_id). Use to translate encounter_id ↔ encounter_name, list bosses in a zone, or filter facts to current-tier encounters. Only active (non-frozen) zones are included.
tables.ai_summary: Encounter dimension. Join on encounter_id to get the boss name when a fact table only has encounter_id, or join on zone_id to filter to a specific raid zone. Active zones only — old tier encounters are excluded.
example_questions: List all bosses in the current raid zone.
What is the boss name for encounter_id = X?
Which encounters are available on Mythic difficulty?
not_recommended_for: Old tier / frozen encounter lookups — those are excluded.
Difficulty-specific keys — difficulty is not part of the primary key here; join on encounter_id only.

columns.name: difficulty_names
columns.description: Available difficulty names for this encounter (e.g. [Normal, Heroic, Mythic]).

tables.grain: One row per guild roster character.
One row per raid zone with guild progression ranks.
One row per active live raid roster entry from Google Sheets.
One row per player identity for attendance across tracked raids.

columns.name: player_realm
columns.description: Realm resolved from WCL actor roster, or `unknown`

tables.grain: One row per player identity, role, boss encounter, zone, and difficulty across kill pulls.
One row per player character equipped item slot.
One row per player character media payload.
One row per player death joined to boss fight context.
One row per player, region, season, and Mythic+ dungeon.

columns.name: best_short_name
columns.description: Short dungeon name for the best run

columns.name: highest_key_level
columns.description: Highest key level in exported payload

columns.name: best_key_level
columns.description: Key level for selected best run

columns.name: best_timed
columns.description: Whether selected best run was timed

tables.grain: One row per Raider.IO Mythic+ run record.

columns.name: timed
columns.description: Whether the run was completed in time

tables.grain: One row per Raider.IO player score snapshot.
tables.description: Time series of Raider.IO score snapshots per character. One row per nightly snapshot per (player_identity_key, region, season). History starts at the first successful ingestion — it is NOT season-start history unless ingestion began at season start. Use this for score-trend questions; for the latest snapshot only, use gold_player_mplus_summary.
tables.ai_summary: Mythic+ score time series. Use for "who is improving in M+", "score trend for player X", or "rate of score change over the last N days". Snapshots are nightly when ingestion runs.
example_questions: Who has the largest M+ score gain in the last 30 days?
Show player X's score trend this season.
Which players have stopped improving (no score change in two weeks)?
When did player X first cross 3000 score?

metrics.name: score_delta
metrics.definition: score_all minus the prior snapshot's score_all for the same (player_identity_key, season).
metrics.caveats: Computed at query time via window functions; a flat history may indicate Raider.IO crawl failure rather than no progress.

not_recommended_for: Latest snapshot lookups — use gold_player_mplus_summary which already filters to latest.
Run-level analysis — this is score-only; for runs use gold_player_mplus_run_history.
Treating start-of-history as start-of-season — history begins at first ingestion.

columns.name: score_all
columns.description: Overall Raider.IO score at this snapshot.

columns.name: score_dps
columns.description: DPS role score at this snapshot.

columns.name: score_healer
columns.description: Healer role score at this snapshot.

columns.name: score_tank
columns.description: Tank role score at this snapshot.

columns.name: raiderio_last_crawled_at
columns.description: Raider.IO crawl timestamp from the source payload

tables.grain: One row per player identity, region, and Raider.IO season.
tables.description: Per-player current Mythic+ snapshot derived from Raider.IO. One row per (player_identity_key, region, season). Includes overall and role scores, world/region/realm/guild ranks, run counts (timed and untimed), highest key levels, and a single "best run" highlight. For score-over-time use gold_player_mplus_score_history; for per-dungeon coverage use gold_player_mplus_dungeon_breakdown; for full run logs use gold_player_mplus_run_history.
tables.ai_summary: Per-player current Mythic+ scoreboard from Raider.IO. Use for "who has the highest M+ score", "guild M+ ranking", or per-role score comparisons. Filter to non-zero score_all to scope to active players. For trend questions use gold_player_mplus_score_history.
example_questions: Who has the highest current Mythic+ score?
List the guild Mythic+ ranking by overall score.
Which players have the highest tank role score?
What is the best timed key level recorded by any guild member?

metrics.name: score_all
metrics.definition: Overall Raider.IO season score for the character.
metrics.caveats: Null/zero for inactive characters; filter `score_all > 0` to scope to active.

metrics.name: highest_timed_level
metrics.definition: Highest key level the character timed in this season.
metrics.caveats: Untimed runs do not contribute; see highest_untimed_level for a separate signal.

not_recommended_for: Joining to raid facts on player_name alone — Mythic+ identity uses (player_name, realm_slug).
Score trend over time — use gold_player_mplus_score_history.
Per-dungeon coverage — use gold_player_mplus_dungeon_breakdown.

columns.name: player_identity_key
columns.description: Lowercase player_name:unknown:realm_slug identity key (Mythic+ has no class context).

columns.name: snapshot_at
columns.description: Ingestion timestamp for the latest score snapshot

columns.name: world_rank
columns.description: Overall world rank if returned by Raider.IO

columns.name: guild_mplus_rank
columns.description: Guild rank by current-season overall score

columns.name: guild_mplus_rank_total
columns.description: Number of guild players with non-zero current-season score

columns.name: total_runs
columns.description: Deduped recent/best runs exported for this character

columns.name: most_common_key_level
columns.description: Most common key level in exported run payload

columns.name: most_common_key_count
columns.description: Count at the most common key level

columns.name: best_run_dungeon
columns.description: Dungeon for the selected best run

columns.name: best_run_level
columns.description: Key level for the selected best run

columns.name: best_run_timed
columns.description: Whether the selected best run was timed

tables.grain: One row per player, region, season, and Mythic+ week.

columns.name: highest_key_level
columns.description: Highest key level completed that week

columns.name: most_common_key_level
columns.description: Most common key level that week

tables.grain: One row per player identity and role.
tables.description: Per-player, per-role aggregate of all kill fights tracked. Use this for "best throughput by player", "average parse for player X", and ranked leaderboards. The grain is (player_identity_key, role) — players who raid as multiple roles will have one row per role.
tables.ai_summary: Aggregate per-player throughput and parse leaderboard. Use for "best DPS overall", "average parse for player X", or "top healers by HPS". For per-boss leaderboards, prefer gold_player_boss_performance. For per-fight detail, use fact_player_fight_performance directly.
example_questions: Who has the best average parse percentile this tier?
List the top 10 DPS by best single-fight throughput.
What is player X's average HPS across all kill fights?
Which healers have the highest average rank percent?

metrics.name: avg_throughput_per_second
metrics.definition: Mean of role-aware per-second throughput across all kill fights for this (player, role).
metrics.caveats: Null when WCL had no ranking entry. Don't bucket null as zero.

metrics.name: avg_rank_percent
metrics.definition: Mean WCL parse percentile (0–100) across kill fights.
metrics.caveats: Recent reports may be missing parse data; the value lags ingestion.

not_recommended_for: Per-fight analysis — this is already aggregated, use fact_player_fight_performance for fights.
Counting attendance — use gold_player_attendance.
Comparing players across roles directly — DPS and HPS are not interchangeable.

columns.name: realm
columns.description: Realm resolved from WCL actor roster, or `unknown`

columns.name: avg_throughput_per_second
columns.description: Average role-aware throughput (DPS for dps/tank, HPS for healer); null when WCL ranking amount is unavailable

columns.name: best_throughput_per_second
columns.description: Single-fight best role-aware throughput (DPS for dps/tank, HPS for healer); null when WCL ranking amount is unavailable

columns.name: avg_rank_percent
columns.description: Average WCL parse percentile, role-aware (DPS parse / HPS parse)

columns.name: best_rank_percent
columns.description: Highest single-fight parse, role-aware (DPS parse / HPS parse)

tables.grain: One row per completed raid feat achievement for a character.
One row per player identity with all-time death statistics.

columns.name: most_common_killing_blow
columns.description: Ability that killed this player most

tables.grain: One row per player and stable player-detail display scope.
tables.description: Per-player survivability rank within a display scope (zone, boss, difficulty), where rank 1 is the best (lowest deaths-per-kill). Use this for "who has the best survival rate", "where does player X rank in survivability", or "top 5 survival players this tier". Pair with gold_wipe_survival_discipline for the wipe-discipline view (deaths on wipes, defensives, healthstones).
tables.ai_summary: Player survivability leaderboard within a display scope. Rank 1 = best (lowest deaths_per_kill); the percentile field is derived from rank. Filter on (zone_name, boss_name, difficulty_label) to scope; use the literal string `All` for cross-scope rows.
example_questions: Who has the best survivability this tier?
List the top 5 most survivable players on Mythic <boss>.
Where does player X rank in survivability?
Which players have the worst deaths-per-kill ratio?

metrics.name: deaths_per_kill
metrics.definition: deaths / max(kills, 1) within scope.
metrics.caveats: A player with kills=0 will not appear in this table.

metrics.name: survivability_rank_percentile
metrics.definition: 100 * (1 - (survivability_rank - 1) / max(survivability_rank_total - 1, 1)).
metrics.caveats: Higher is better; tied ranks share a percentile.

not_recommended_for: Wipe-side analysis — use gold_wipe_survival_discipline.
Per-fight death events — use fact_player_events.
Comparing scopes without a (zone_name, boss_name, difficulty_label) filter.

columns.name: encounter_id
columns.description: Encounter id when the display boss scope maps to one encounter; null for all-boss or ambiguous all-zone scopes

columns.name: difficulty
columns.description: Difficulty id when the display difficulty scope maps to one id; null for all-difficulty or ambiguous scopes

columns.name: deaths_per_kill
columns.description: Deaths divided by kills; lower is better.

columns.name: survivability_rank
columns.description: Rank in scope; 1 is best survivability.

columns.name: survivability_rank_total
columns.description: Number of ranked players in scope.

tables.grain: One row per player on an instrumented pull with utility usage counts.
One row per current raid-team preparation identity.
tables.description: Per-raider current-tier preparation scoreboard. Combines food, flask/phial, weapon-enhancement, and combat-potion coverage on kill fights into a 0–100 readiness_score with a categorical readiness_label (watch/steady/strong) and a `weakest_signal_label` indicating where to improve. Combat-potion usage is displayed for all roles but contributes to scoring only for DPS (per project convention). Scope is current tier only — historical consumable usage lives in fact_player_fight_performance.
tables.ai_summary: Current-tier preparation scoreboard. Per-raider 0–100 readiness_score composed of food, flask/phial, weapon-enhancement, and (DPS-only) combat potion coverage on kill fights. Use for "who has poor consumable usage", "who is least prepared", or "what is player X's weakest consumable signal". Combat potions display for all roles but only contribute to scoring for DPS.
example_questions: Who has poor consumable usage on the current tier?
Which raiders need to improve their flask/phial coverage?
List players in the `watch` readiness bucket.
What is player X's weakest preparation signal?
Who has the highest readiness_score this tier?

metrics.name: readiness_score
metrics.definition: 0–100 composite of food, flask, weapon, and (DPS-only) combat-potion coverage on current-tier kills.
metrics.caveats: Only kill fights are counted; players with no current-tier kills will have has_current_tier_data=false.

metrics.name: food_rate
metrics.definition: Share of current-tier kills with a recognised food buff active.

metrics.name: flask_rate
metrics.definition: Share of current-tier kills with a flask or phial active.

not_recommended_for: Historical (cross-tier) consumable usage — use fact_player_fight_performance.
Wipe-side discipline — use gold_wipe_survival_discipline (defensives, healthstones, potions on wipes).
Counting raid attendance independently — use gold_player_attendance.

columns.name: identity_key
columns.description: Character or published override identity key

columns.name: player_class
columns.description: Latest known class or roster class

columns.name: roster_source
columns.description: Source roster used to generate this row.

columns.name: attendance_rate_pct
columns.description: Current-tier raid nights present / raid nights tracked × 100

columns.name: raids_present
columns.description: Current-tier raid nights with at least one kill row

columns.name: food_rate
columns.description: Share (0–1) of current-tier kill fights with a recognised food buff.

columns.name: flask_rate
columns.description: Share (0–1) of current-tier kill fights with a flask or phial.

columns.name: weapon_rate
columns.description: Share (0–1) of current-tier kill fights with a weapon enhancement applied.

columns.name: combat_potion_rate
columns.description: Share (0–1) of current-tier kill fights with a combat-potion cast (DPS-only signal for scoring; displayed for all roles).

columns.name: spec
columns.description: Most recent specialisation observed for this identity, or empty.

columns.name: latest_avg_item_level
columns.description: Most recent equipped average item level.

columns.name: latest_kill_date
columns.description: ISO date of the most recent current-tier kill, or empty if none.

columns.name: recent_food_names
columns.description: Latest non-empty current-tier food buff names, or empty string

columns.name: recent_flask_names
columns.description: Latest non-empty current-tier flask/phial names, or empty string

columns.name: recent_weapon_names
columns.description: Latest non-empty current-tier weapon enhancement names, or empty string

columns.name: recent_combat_potion_names
columns.description: Latest non-empty classified combat potion names. If potion usage exists but the event name is unavailable, `Combat potion used`; otherwise empty string

columns.name: character_names
columns.description: Pipe-delimited characters included in the identity

columns.name: override_label
columns.description: Identity-pool override applied to this row.

tables.grain: One row per first kill contributing to cumulative progression timeline.
One row per WarcraftLogs report.
tables.description: Per-raid-night rollup. One row per WCL report — covers report timing, zone, primary difficulty, total pulls, kills, wipes, and unique bosses engaged. Use this for "what happened on raid night X" or weekly activity questions. For per-encounter progression use gold_boss_progression; for per-week rollups use gold_weekly_activity.
tables.ai_summary: Raid-night summary table. Use to ask "how did raid go on date X", "how many pulls did we do this week", or "what's our kill count over time". Joins to fact_player_fight_performance and fact_player_events on report_code.
example_questions: How many pulls did we do last raid night?
Show kill count by raid night for the last month.
Which raid night had the most wipes?
How many unique bosses did we engage this week?

metrics.name: kills_per_pull
metrics.definition: boss_kills / max(total_pulls, 1).
metrics.caveats: A blunt efficiency signal; depends heavily on which bosses were attempted.

not_recommended_for: Per-encounter progression — use gold_boss_progression.
Player-level analysis — this table has no player columns.
Weekly rollups — use gold_weekly_activity for ISO-week aggregation.

columns.name: report_title
columns.description: Report title as set in WCL.

columns.name: total_pulls
columns.description: Total pulls (kills + wipes) on this report.

columns.name: boss_kills
columns.description: Number of successful boss kills on this report.

columns.name: total_wipes
columns.description: Number of wipes on this report.

columns.name: total_fight_seconds
columns.description: Sum of active fight durations across all pulls.

columns.name: unique_bosses_engaged
columns.description: Distinct bosses pulled on this report.

columns.name: unique_bosses_killed
columns.description: Distinct bosses killed on this report.

tables.grain: One row per active raid team member.
One row per raid activity week.

columns.name: raid_nights
columns.description: Number of unique reports that week

columns.name: total_boss_kills
columns.description: Sum of boss kills across all reports

columns.name: total_raid_seconds
columns.description: Total active fight time in seconds

tables.grain: One row per player, pull, and tracked cooldown ability with scored capacity and usage.
One row per player and stable Wipe Analysis dashboard scope.
tables.description: Per-(player, scope) wipe-discipline diagnosis. Each row evaluates a player on one stable scope (typically all-zones, a specific zone, or a specific (zone, boss, difficulty)). It scores how often the player dies on wipes, whether they used personal defensives, healthstones, and combat health potions, and combines those into a 0–100 survival_discipline_score. Use this to answer "who is dying most on wipes", "who has poor cooldown discipline", "what should player X improve". For raid-team unused cooldown capacity (raid CDs / externals) use gold_wipe_cooldown_utilization.
tables.ai_summary: Player-side wipe diagnosis with a 0–100 survival_discipline_score (higher is better). Use for "who has the worst survival discipline", "who is not using defensives", "what should player X improve". Pair with fact_player_events for raw death detail and with gold_wipe_cooldown_utilization for raid-team-level cooldown coverage.
example_questions: Who has the worst survival discipline this tier?
Which players never use healthstones on wipes?
What is player X's top improvement area?
Who has poor defensive cooldown usage relative to their class?
Which abilities most often kill player X on wipes?

metrics.name: survival_discipline_score
metrics.definition: 0–100 composite of personal defensives, healthstones, potions, and death pressure on wipes; higher is better.
metrics.caveats: Class-baselined for defensives; presence-normalised. Null/zero capacity is handled via defensive_tracking_status.

metrics.name: deaths_per_wipe
metrics.definition: wipe_deaths / wipe_pulls_tracked.
metrics.caveats: Sensitive to small samples; check wipe_pulls_tracked before drawing conclusions.

metrics.name: defensive_usage_rate
metrics.definition: defensive_casts / tracked_defensive_capacity * 100.
metrics.caveats: Null when no tracked capacity for class/spec.

metrics.name: survival_grade
metrics.definition: S/A/B/C/D/E/F letter grade for survival_discipline_score within the same (zone_name, boss_name, difficulty_label) partition. Computed as a dense-rank-based relative bucketing — single-distinct-score partitions receive S; otherwise the highest distinct score gets S, the lowest F, and the middle ranks are bucketed by middle_relative = (rank-2)/(n-3) with thresholds 0.8/0.6/0.4/0.2 mapping to A/B/C/D, else E.
metrics.caveats: Identical scores share a grade; small partitions (n_distinct <= 3) collapse the middle to A only.

not_recommended_for: Counting kills — this is wipe-discipline, not kill performance.
Raid-team cooldown coverage — use gold_wipe_cooldown_utilization.
Per-pull death events — use fact_player_events.
Comparing across all-scope and specific-scope rows in the same query without a scope filter.

columns.name: realm
columns.description: Realm resolved from WCL actor roster, or `unknown`.

columns.name: encounter_id
columns.description: Encounter id; null for all-boss scopes

columns.name: difficulty
columns.description: WCL difficulty; null for all-difficulty scopes

columns.name: wipe_pulls_tracked
columns.description: Instrumented wipe pulls where the player was present

columns.name: wipe_deaths
columns.description: Player deaths on tracked wipe pulls

columns.name: first_deaths
columns.description: Wipe deaths that were first in the pull

columns.name: early_deaths
columns.description: Wipe deaths within the first minute

columns.name: kill_deaths
columns.description: Player deaths on kill pulls in scope

columns.name: kills_tracked
columns.description: Kill pulls where the player was present

columns.name: deaths_per_kill
columns.description: `kill_deaths / kills_tracked`; null when no kills are tracked

columns.name: pulls_with_tracked_defensive_capacity
columns.description: Wipe pulls with scored personal defensive capacity

columns.name: defensive_missed_casts
columns.description: Scored possible defensive casts left unused

columns.name: healthstone_uses
columns.description: Wipe deaths with healthstone use before death

columns.name: potion_uses
columns.description: Wipe deaths with health potion use before death

columns.name: no_healthstone_deaths
columns.description: Wipe deaths where the player did NOT use a healthstone before dying.

columns.name: no_health_potion_deaths
columns.description: Wipe deaths where the player did NOT use a health potion before dying.

columns.name: defensive_usage_rate
columns.description: `defensive_casts / tracked_defensive_capacity * 100`; null when no defensive capacity is tracked

columns.name: healthstone_usage_rate
columns.description: `healthstone_uses / wipe_deaths * 100`; null when no wipe deaths exist

columns.name: potion_usage_rate
columns.description: `potion_uses / wipe_deaths * 100`; null when no wipe deaths exist

columns.name: death_pressure_score
columns.description: 0–100 component score for avoiding wipe deaths

columns.name: defensive_component_score
columns.description: 0–100 class-baselined defensive component; unknown and no-capacity states receive a neutral component while `defensive_tracking_status` preserves the distinction

columns.name: defensive_class_baseline_pct
columns.description: Scope/class median defensive usage rate for tracked-capacity players

columns.name: defensive_class_delta_pct
columns.description: Player's defensive_usage_rate minus the class baseline; positive = above class median.

columns.name: weighted_failure_points
columns.description: Internal weighted sum of survival failure components used to derive survival_discipline_score; lower is better.

columns.name: survival_discipline_score
columns.description: Absolute 0–100 consistency score; higher is better

columns.name: top_improvement_area
columns.description: Lowest component label, or `—` when all components are strong

columns.name: top_missing_category
columns.description: Component label most responsible for the failure score (e.g. defensives, healthstones, potions, deaths).

columns.name: has_defensive_capacity_tracked
columns.description: True when the player has scoreable defensive capacity for this scope; false when class/spec has no tracked defensives or no pulls had capacity tracking.

columns.name: most_common_killing_blow
columns.description: Most frequent killing-blow ability against this player on wipes in scope (empty when no wipe deaths).

columns.name: survival_grade
columns.description: Per-scope relative letter grade derived from survival_discipline_score within the same (zone_name, boss_name, difficulty_label) partition; S = best, F = worst.

tables.grain: One row per player death on an instrumented wipe pull.
